facts.text: Ansonia was founded in 1844 by merchant Anson Green Phelps, who named the city after himself and steered it toward copper and brass manufacturing.
In 1851, the Ansonia Clock Company began here, becoming one of the first American clock makers to export its timepieces worldwide.
A canal was dug to harness the Naugatuck River’s power, driving the factories that turned Ansonia into a bustling industrial village.
Ansonia’s historic Elm Street district offers a walking tour that highlights Victorian and Queen Anne homes, a public library, and the Anna Sewell Memorial Fountain.
The city sits on the Waterbury Branch of the Metro-North Railroad, making it a stop on the commuter line that connects to New York City.
